_article: XXXIX.2 — bandits run out of food, expand or contract territory, raise or lower raid cadence. XXXIX.3 — the player intersects trajectories (bandits are already mid-campaign).
_description: Momentum schema for a BanditGroup (or hostile BeastPopulation used as bandit-equivalent) trajectory entity. A bandit group is not a static spawn. It has a food/supply trajectory, a desperation level, a raid cadence, a territory size, and a prey density. When supplies are high, raids are cautious and opportunistic. When supplies run low, desperation rises and raids become bold and frequent — until the group is destroyed, dispersed, or resupplied by a successful raid.

_design_notes.why_this_matters: The user's example: 'Bandits running out of food.' This is the trajectory that makes a road dangerous not because bandits are 'spawned there' but because a specific group, with a specific supply situation, is being forced to raid. The player who understands this can exploit it (wait for them to starve), redirect it (feed them to lower desperation — at a cost), or end it (destroy the group).
_design_notes.coupling: Feeds: caravan_momentum (ambush threshold), settlement_graph (raid events), travel_graph (segment threat_exposure), rumor_momentum (raid news spreads). Fed by: regional economy (prey density = caravans + villages).
perceivable_without_notification_example: A burned-out wagon off the road, weeks old — a bandit attack happened here.
Villagers no longer travel the east road alone — the innkeeper mentions it offhand.
Smoke from a campfire in the hills where no lawful person would camp.
A merchant arrives in town wounded and stripped of goods — the bandits are active and bold.

schema.group_id: string — matches a BanditGroup entity id

current_state.desperation: float 0.0–1.0 — driven by supplies; high desperation = bold raids
current_state.raid_cadence_ticks: int — average ticks between raids (low when desperate, high when comfortable)
current_state.last_raid_tick: int — when the last raid occurred (drives perceivable 'recent attack' cues)
current_state.territory_polygon_id: string — the region entity the group currently operates in
current_state.prey_density: float 0.0–1.0 — caravan + village traffic in territory (drives raid success and supply replenishment)
current_state.morale: float 0.0–1.0 — group cohesion (low morale = desertion, splintering)

rate_of_change.supplies_per_tick: float — negative (consumption) + positive (successful raids, foraging, external supply)
rate_of_change.desperation_per_tick: float — inversely correlated with supplies; rises sharply below 0.2 supplies
rate_of_change.territory_size_per_tick: float — expands when comfortable and unopposed; contracts when pressured
rate_of_change.headcount_per_tick: int — recruitment (from desperate locals) - attrition (raids, starvation, desertion)

components.raid_income: float — supplies gained per successful raid (driven by prey_density and desperation)
components.desperation_pressure: float — rises as supplies fall; raises raid_cadence and boldness

drivers: string — e.g. 'black_wind bandits: 14 headcount, supplies 0.31 (falling), desperation 0.68 (rising), raid_cadence 9000 ticks (was 18000 last season — they are getting bold), last_raid 4000 ticks ago (a caravan on the zhao-chu road), territory east hills, prey_density 0.42 (the road is still traveled enough to be worth raiding)'

thresholds.name: starvation_crisis
thresholds.consequence: desperation maxes; raid_cadence halves; bandits attack targets they would normally avoid (patrols, walled settlements); morale begins to break. Either a successful raid resupplies them, or the group starves and disperses (some die, some desert, some become lone raiders).

thresholds.name: raid_blitz
thresholds.consequence: group commits to aggressive raiding; multiple caravans/settlements hit in short window; this is the perceivable 'the road has become dangerous' period. Regional economy_momentum may enter scarcity. Patrols may be dispatched.

thresholds.name: dispersal
thresholds.consequence: group ceases to exist as a coherent threat; survivors scatter (some may reform elsewhere, some may join other groups, some may return to village life under false identities). The territory becomes safe — until a new group forms.

thresholds.name: expansion
thresholds.condition: supplies > 0.7 AND morale > 0.7 AND headcount growing
thresholds.consequence: group expands territory; may absorb smaller groups; may establish a permanent camp (becomes a Stronghold entity); may begin extortion (protection deals with villages). This is how bandits become warlords in Er Gen canon.

interruptible_by: Patrol or sect suppression (lowers headcount, raises morale-break risk)
Player or NPC clears the camp (ends trajectory)
Village pays protection (lowers raid_cadence on that village; raises supplies)
Famine or weather (lowers forage_income; accelerates starvation_crisis)
Recruitment by a faction (bandits become soldiers; trajectory transforms)
schema.perceivable_without_notification: string — REQUIRED (Art XXXIX.5). e.g. 'A burnt wagon off the Zhao–Chu road, the attack less than a week old. The village innkeeper, without prompting, warns travelers not to take the east road alone. Smoke rises from the hills at dusk where no farmstead sits. A merchant limped in last night, stripped of goods, talking of fourteen bandits — he counted them before they let him run.' If empty, FAILS the Momentum Test.
schema.compiled_into: travel_graph (segment threat_exposure); settlement_graph (raid events); influence_map (bandit influence zone)
schema.canon_ref: INFERRED (Art XV) — bandits are a constant low-canon presence in Er Gen's mortal world; specific groups authored for Chapter 1
